# 1.0 Support Unit Test

This document provides unit tests for the 1.0 Support chat mode. The tests are designed to validate the basic functionality and exception handling of the chat mode.
use xUnit for unit testing in C#.
use Substitute for mocking dependencies.

## Test Case: Basic Functionality

```csharp
using Xunit;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading.Tasks;   

namespace SupportUnitTest;

public class BasicFunctionalityTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void TestBasicFunctionality()
    {
        // Arrange
        var expected = "Hello, World!";
        var actual = "Hello, World!";

        // Act & Assert
        Assert.Equal(expected, actual);
    }
}
```

## Test Case: Exception Handling

```csharp
using Xunit;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

namespace SupportUnitTest;

public class ExceptionHandlingTests
{
    [Fact]
    public void TestExceptionHandling()
    {
        // Arrange
        Action action = () => throw new InvalidOperationException("Test exception");

        // Act & Assert
        var exception = Assert.Throws<InvalidOperationException>(action);
        Assert.Equal("Test exception", exception.Message);
    }
}
```

## Test Case: Mocking Dependencies

```csharp
using Xunit;
using NSubstitute;
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Linq;
using System.Text;
using System.Threading.Tasks;

namespace SupportUnitTest;

public class MockingDependenciesTests
{
    public interface IDependency
    {
        string GetData();
    }

    [Fact]
    public void TestMockingDependencies()
    {
        // Arrange
        var mockDependency = Substitute.For<IDependency>();
        mockDependency.GetData().Returns("Mocked Data");

        // Act
        var result = mockDependency.GetData();

        // Assert
        Assert.Equal("Mocked Data", result);
    }
}

```
